response:
Short answer: azr-compliance-v3 performs Event Grid bulk remediation as a dedicated, server‑side, database-backed bulk-run system with a single timer worker that snapshots targets, persists per‑item state for UI continuity, and includes worker/DB safeguards so it can scale and recover.

How it works (key points):
- Dedicated bulk tables: bulk runs and run items live in Postgres (event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs, event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items) instead of reusing the generic sync_queue.
- Snapshot of targets: a bulk start captures the full deployable subscription set from the shared Event Grid coverage SQL so the worker has a stable workset to operate on at scale.
- Single timer worker + advisory lock: a 15‑second timer worker (ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation) is guarded by a Postgres advisory lock so only one worker processes a run at a time.
- Safe start/merge semantics: the bulk start endpoint will reuse an existing active queued/running run rather than creating duplicates, and avoids recomputing deployable targets if an active run exists.
- Persistent per‑item status for UX: item statuses are persisted so the frontend can POST once to start the run, then poll GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest and merge persisted item states into the UI (Latest Bulk Remediation panel, per‑subscription badges).
- Robustness / recovery: stale running items are requeued back to pending on worker restart so interrupted runs can recover; there’s a recency index on run_items for efficient ordering and paging.
- Fleet interaction knobs: worker has configurable Fleet polling/env knobs to manage downstream Fleet/API polling; the server mediates Fleet calls for bulk work when appropriate.
- UX/behaviour hardening: ordering by recency, correct paging/consistency for the panel, and making the “View Active Bulk Run” a read/refocus action to avoid duplicate POSTs.

Result: a scalable, observable, restart‑resilient bulk remediation pipeline that snapshots targets, processes items with a single coordinated worker, persists detailed item state for UI continuity, and includes DB and worker-level hardening for production scale.
context_injected:
--- Episode 1 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.815) ---
Implemented dedicated server-side Event Grid bulk remediation in azr-compliance-v3. Design choice: do not reuse sync_queue; instead use Postgres-backed tables event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items plus a dedicated 15-second timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation guarded by its own advisory lock. Bulk start endpoint reuses an active queued/running run, snapshots the full deployable subscription set from shared Event Grid coverage SQL, and persists latest-run item statuses for page refresh continuity. EventGridDetails now calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk once, polls GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest, merges latest item statuses into visible deployable cards, and shows a Latest Bulk Remediation panel.

--- Episode 2 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.6778) ---
azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediation was switched from a browser-side Fleet API call to a backend-mediated flow because Fleet requires application permission. The backend now acquires a ClientSecretCredential token for scope api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f/.default and calls https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription, polling the returned statusUrl until completion. The frontend calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure instead of Fleet directly.

--- Episode 3 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.6764) ---
On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, integrated Event Grid remediation on the Event Grid Details page as a browser-side Fleet Platform call rather than a backend proxy because the browser reliably has the operator MSAL Bearer token while the backend, behind APIM, may not retain the raw Authorization credential in deployed environments. The page now uses metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi), requests an optional Fleet-specific scope via VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE, calls POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_BASE_URL, polls statusUrl until terminal runtimeStatus, treats succeeded/already_exists as success, maps 404 to not_found, and maps 409 to not_eligible. UI adds per-subscription Ensure Event Grid and Ensure All Deployable actions plus local remediation state badges.

--- Episode 4 [codex 2026-04-10] (similarity: 0.6409) ---
PR #76 review feedback on server-side Event Grid bulk remediation produced four concrete follow-up fixes: avoid computing deployable targets when an active bulk run already exists; order latest bulk remediation items by recency instead of ascending id; source the BulkRemediationPanel run/loading state from the same paged query as its items to avoid mixed-run rendering; and make the 'View Active Bulk Run' button a true read/refocus action instead of issuing another POST.

--- Episode 5 [codex 2026-03-27] (similarity: 0.6248) ---
On 2026-03-27, live prod validation after a large event burst in azr-compliance-v3 showed the register path and queue handoff were working, but sync_bulk_automation_execute was delayed behind serial queue processing until 21:07Z. After it ran, post-21:07 transport was clean: /api/bulk-operations/register returned 201 four times, /api/bulk-operations/callback returned 200 seven times, and there were zero response 500s in api_audit_all for that post-21:07 window. Bulk item state since 19:30Z then settled to a mix of succeeded, invalid, queued, and failed: policy duplicates were suppressed as invalid with reason 'A successful policy/AMA create already completed recently...', but a smaller set of policy items for subscription e4c18fc7-011a-44ee-a1d0-5fad9a5a007a still failed with Azure InvalidUpdateRemediationRequest on active remediations, confirming the remaining active-remediation guard needs to live in the downstream remediation app rather than azr-compliance-v3. Alert items still had a residual queued/failed subset with no last_error in bulk_automation_items.

--- Episode 6 [codex 2026-04-10] (similarity: 0.6236) ---
Created follow-up hardening for Event Grid bulk remediation after PR #77 review comments. The fix adds forward migration 58 for a recency index on event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items(run_id, updated_at DESC, id DESC), adds bulk-worker-specific Fleet polling env knobs, and requeues stale running items back to pending so a worker restart can recover interrupted runs.

--- Episode 7 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.6179) ---
On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, redesigned Event Grid Details to distinguish deployment inventory from remediation coverage. GetEventGridDeployments now returns metadata.coverage with deployable_missing_event_grid_count, missing_monitoring_count, and arrays for deployable_subscriptions vs out_of_scope_subscriptions, derived from active customer subscriptions, monitoring entitlement, and Rackspace-managed Event Grid inventory. The frontend EventGridDetails page now shows explicit remediation buckets: 'Ready For Event Grid Deployment' for monitoring-enabled subscriptions missing Event Grid, and 'Monitoring Required First' for subscriptions outside monitoring scope. This resolves ambiguity in the prior 'Subscriptions Without Event Grid' card, which only reflected monitoring-entitled scope.

--- Episode 8 [codex 2026-03-27] (similarity: 0.6125) ---
On 2026-03-27 in azr-compliance-v3 develop, fixed two bulk automation issues. First, RegisterBulkOperation was inserting compatibility rows as status=sent with selected=false and never queueing sync_bulk_automation_execute; changed it to queue status, selected=true, and immediate enqueue with self-healing sync operation registration. Second, ExecuteBulkOperation now validates policy create items before publish: it blocks create when current local comparison no longer needs remediation, when the assignment already exists with no current gap, or when a recent successful policy/AMA create tracker row exists within the dedupe window. Prod validation showed remediation_tasks is empty in prod, so active-remediation prevention in this repo must rely on current comparison/assignment state and recent policy tracker success rather than a remediation task ledger.

--- Episode 9 [codex 2026-03-23] (similarity: 0.6004) ---
On 2026-03-23 in azr-fleet-platform PR #67, three additional Event Grid review findings were validated and fixed in commit 5cd48c3. Fixes: targeted Event Grid DELETE now validates that the subscription belongs to the URL customer before starting the delete orchestration while still skipping monitoring-tier eligibility; ensure_event_subscription_single now writes a Cosmos EventGridSubscriptionCustomerMismatch event before returning a customer/subscription mismatch failure; orchestrate_onboarding changed event_grid_result['skipped'] back to a boolean and added skipped_no_monitoring as the explicit count, with finalize_run updated to carry both fields. Targeted Event Grid tests passed and all new review threads were replied to and resolved.

--- Episode 10 [codex 2026-03-30] (similarity: 0.5828) ---
On 2026-03-30 in azr-compliance-v3 develop, corrected a bad bulk-operations UI fix. The user did not want parent policy invalid/failed diagnostics copied to child alert rows. Removed the child-alert inheritance changes from web-dashboard/src/pages/BulkOperations.jsx and instead added backend backfill in backend-py/GetBulkOperationItems/__init__.py so invalid policy rows with blank last_error derive a reason from subscription_compliance_comparison and azure_policy_assignments. Added integration test test_get_bulk_operation_items_backfills_invalid_policy_reason and pushed commit ac0fc372 to develop for PR #54.
